steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The goal is to make and support a claim about the inspiration for kumiko patterns. A strong answer must (1) state what inspires the patterns and (2) provide specific evidence from the notes that backs that claim.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — The tortoiseshell-like overlapping hexagons of the kikko pattern are inspired by wooden strips from nature.: Misidentifies the source of inspiration as the construction material rather than nature. Wrong claim. ✗
  • Choice B — Often resembling elements from nature, kumiko patterns are formed by fitting thin strips of wood together without the use of nails.: Focuses on how kumiko is built, not what inspires its patterns. Fails the inspiration requirement. ✗
  • Choice C — The kumiko woodworking technique is used to create latticed panels with geometric designs, like overlapping hexagons, out of thin strips of wood.: Describes kumiko patterns without ever stating what inspires them. Incomplete. ✗
  • Choice D — Like the seigaiha pattern's curved fan shapes, which resemble ocean waves, many of the geometric patterns used in kumiko designs are inspired by elements from nature.: States the claim (geometric patterns inspired by nature) and supports it with a specific example from the notes (the seigaiha pattern's curved fan shapes resembling ocean waves). ✓
That matches Choice D — Like the seigaiha pattern's curved fan shapes, which resemble ocean waves, many of the geometric patterns used in kumiko designs are inspired by elements from nature..
